tossups.question: This artist was portrayed by David Elias Callaham in the 2021 parody film America: The Motion Picture. They were heavily inspired by garden villas and eventually designed a museum near the location of said villas. This architect put his degree from the Harvard University Graduate School of Design into the construction of the (*) John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum in Boston. Sketches made by this artist can be found in the very building they are meant to outline. Glass pyramids, big or small, are a central theme in his work. For ten points, name this Chinese-American architect who died at 102 and designed the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame, the Louvre Pyramid, and the East Building of the National Gallery of Art.
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist was portrayed by David Elias Callaham in the 2021 parody film America: The Motion Picture. They were heavily inspired by garden villas and eventually designed a museum near the location of said villas. This architect put his degree from the Harvard University Graduate School of Design into the construction of the (*) John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum in Boston. Sketches made by this artist can be found in the very building they are meant to outline. Glass pyramids, big or small, are a central theme in his work. For ten points, name this Chinese-American architect who died at 102 and designed the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame, the Louvre Pyramid, and the East Building of the National Gallery of Art.

tossups.question: This architect used then-unusual all-glass mullions for the facade of his now-demolished Sundrome at JFK. Walter Roberts used the phrase “just a bunch of towers” to criticize this architect’s initial design for the Pueblo-inspired NCAR Mesa Laboratory. The Myriad Botanical Gardens and Convention Center were constructed as part of this man’s namesake design plan for Oklahoma City. He designed a building in which a “glass tent” made up of two triangles leans against a white rectangular tower on the coast of Lake Erie. His most famous design is for a structure located between the Sully, Denon, and Richelieu Wings of an art museum and, despite popular myth, does not actually have 666 panes. For 10 points, name this architect of the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame and the Louvre Pyramid.
tossups.question_sanitized: This architect used then-unusual all-glass mullions for the facade of his now-demolished Sundrome at JFK. Walter Roberts used the phrase "just a bunch of towers" to criticize this architect's initial design for the Pueblo-inspired NCAR Mesa Laboratory. The Myriad Botanical Gardens and Convention Center were constructed as part of this man's namesake design plan for Oklahoma City. He designed a building in which a "glass tent" made up of two triangles leans against a white rectangular tower on the coast of Lake Erie. His most famous design is for a structure located between the Sully, Denon, and Richelieu Wings of an art museum and, despite popular myth, does not actually have 666 panes. For 10 points, name this architect of the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame and the Louvre Pyramid.

bonuses.leadin: The Howard Hughes Corporation now owns a Honolulu building commissioned by this company with a honeycomb-esque brise soleil (“breez so-LAY”). For 10 points each:
parts: Name this company for whom Eliot Noyes hired Charles and Ray Eames to make the Powers of Ten films. Alexander Calder’s reptilian sculpture Saurien (“sohr-YAWN”) sits under a building nicknamed for this company at 590 Madison Avenue.
IBM rejected this architect’s plans for a skyscraper headquarters in 1969. This architect created a glass “tent” as a symbol of musical expression for a building on Lake Erie.
Noyes commissioned Ludwig Mies van der Rohe’s last American building at the former “IBM Plaza” at 330 North Wabash in this city, whose “Loop” features skyscrapers like the Willis Tower.
answers: IBM [or International Business Machines Corporation; accept IBM Building] (The brise soleil resembles the punched cards that were once ubiquitous in the computing industry.)
I.M. Pei [or Ieoh Ming Pei] (The building is the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame in Cleveland, Ohio.)

packet.name: 10 - Chicago C, Haverford A, Swarthmore A, Miami Ohio A
